hero.label: Case Study
hero.title: WAIS
hero.subtitle: Think in Colors
hero.description: A guided ideation tool designed to help teams think with clarity and psychological safety.

sections.title: The Problem
sections.content: Team brainstorming sessions often suffer from groupthink, dominant voices overshadowing quiet contributors, and a lack of structured thinking. Traditional brainstorming tools don't provide the psychological safety or framework needed for inclusive ideation.

sections.title: Research & Discovery
sections.content: Through user interviews and team observation sessions, I discovered that the biggest barriers to effective brainstorming were:
list: Fear of judgment when sharing unconventional ideas
Lack of visual frameworks to guide thinking

sections.title: The Solution
sections.content: WAIS introduces "thinking modes" — color-coded frameworks that guide teams through different cognitive approaches. Each mode provides structure while maintaining anonymity, ensuring every voice is heard.

sections.title: Reflection
sections.content: Working on WAIS taught me the importance of designing for psychological safety in collaborative tools. The color-coded thinking modes became a powerful visual language that reduced cognitive load and made abstract thinking processes tangible.
